serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one raw lean pork midloin chop weighing approximately 120 g before cooking, providing around 152 kcal, 26.4 g protein, and 4.5 g fat.

nutrient_source.title: Pork midloin chop, raw, lean — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for 'Pork midloin chop, raw, lean' (cr_id F06090032). All core macros and secondary nutrients sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB; estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 10062 (Pork, fresh, loin, center rib (chops), bone-in, separable lean only, raw) as 1.28 g/100g.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate food
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pork midloin chop, raw, lean contains 0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a GI value to pure protein foods with zero carbohydrate content. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition (GL = GI x carbohydrate_g / 100; carbohydrate_g = 0).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (58 mg/100g) and high potassium (362 mg/100g) yield a favourable Na:K ratio aligned with DASH dietary principles.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension may include lean pork midloin chop as a protein source, provided it is prepared without added salt, high-sodium marinades, or sauces; pairing with potassium-rich vegetables further supports blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content means this food exerts no glycaemic load and will not elevate postprandial blood glucose.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lean pork midloin chop is a suitable high-protein, zero-carbohydrate protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; it should be prepared without sugary marinades or glazes and paired with high-fibre vegetables and whole grains to compose a balanced, low-GI meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.71 g/100g) and moderate saturated fat (approximately 1.28 g/100g) with high lean protein content make this a heart-friendly red meat option when consumed in moderation.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease may consume lean pork midloin chop in moderate portions (up to 120 g cooked), prepared by grilling or steaming without added fat, as part of a diet that limits total saturated fat intake to below 10% of total energy.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork is a red meat containing arachidonic acid (omega-6), which can promote pro-inflammatory cytokine production; the lean cut reduces saturated fat but does not eliminate arachidonic acid.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit red meat including lean pork to 1–2 servings per week per EULAR anti-inflammatory dietary guidelines, and pair with anti-inflammatory foods such as fatty fish, walnuts, or turmeric-spiced dishes.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork midloin chop, raw, lean (cr_id F06090032) is a raw, uncooked cut of pork taken from the loin section of the pig, presented bone-in with all visible internal and external separable fat removed. The meat displays a pale pink colour with a firm, dense texture characteristic of lean pork loin. Per 100 g, it provides 127 kcal, 21.99 g protein, 3.71 g total fat (approximately 1.28 g saturated fat), 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 58 mg sodium, 362 mg potassium, 18 mg calcium, 0.65 mg iron, and 69 mg cholesterol. The zero carbohydrate content means it carries no glycaemic load and is suitable for low-carbohydrate, ketogenic, and diabetic dietary patterns. Its high potassium-to-sodium ratio aligns with DASH dietary principles, making it appropriate for individuals managing hypertension. The low total fat and moderate saturated fat content make it one of the leaner red meat options available. Clinically, it is a high-quality complete protein source supporting muscle maintenance and insulin sensitivity. It should be prepared by grilling, steaming, or pan-searing without added fat or high-sodium seasonings to preserve its nutritional advantages. As a red meat, it contains arachidonic acid and should be consumed in moderation by individuals with inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database (cr_id F06090032) with a reported confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat and fatty acid breakdown estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 10062 (closest approved-source equivalent for lean pork loin chop, raw), which is an approved secondary source. All other values are exact matches from HPB.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as pork is a pure animal protein and fat food containing no carbohydrates. Sugar_g is naturally zero by the same rationale. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle meat contains no dietary fibre. Confirmed zero per Singapore HPB Foodo database. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 based on USDA FoodData Central data for lean pork loin (trace amounts below detection threshold). Saturated_fat_g (1.28 g/100g), monounsaturated_fat_g (1.55 g/100g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.38 g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 10062 (Pork, fresh, loin, center rib chops, bone-in, separable lean only, raw) as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown for this entry. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data not reported in the HPB Foodo entry for this specific record; USDA FoodData Central NDB 10062 does contain some of these values but they were not incorporated to avoid mixing source granularity without explicit cross-validation. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0 g/100g); gi_category is set to not_applicable and glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. The template field glycemic_index is set to 0 as a numeric sentinel consistent with the template requirement that glycemic_index be numeric; the gi_category field of not_applicable and gi_source notes make clear that GI is not applicable for this food. This food is not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish; therefore no mandatory non-English local-language names are required under G6 multilingual rules. Halal status is set to false as pork is not halal.
